note: Catalog entries are NOT deep-researched records. They exist so every job is findable. Pay/retirement/transition/crosswalk deliberately absent rather than invented. ASVAB is null for every entry because the Coast Guard has no ASVAB line-score system: each rating's minimum qualification is a sum of raw ASVAB subtest standard scores defined in COMDTINST M1510.10 Enclosure 3, a document not reachable in this session. Clearance is null for every entry because per-rating clearance level (e.g., IS almost certainly requires Secret/Top Secret) was not confirmed against a primary source this session and is flagged rather than guessed.

specialties.name: Aviation Maintenance Technician
specialties.what_it_is: AMTs inspect, maintain, and troubleshoot aircraft engines, propellers, and airframe systems.

specialties.name: Aviation Survival Technician
specialties.what_it_is: ASTs are rescue swimmers who save lives, provide emergency medical support, and maintain survival equipment.

specialties.name: Avionics Electrical Technician
specialties.what_it_is: AETs inspect, service, maintain, troubleshoot, and repair aircraft electronic systems.

specialties.name: Boatswain's Mate
specialties.what_it_is: BMs handle navigation and seamanship aboard Coast Guard cutters and boats.

specialties.name: Culinary Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: CSs handle food preparation, accounting, and logistics for Coast Guard galleys.

specialties.name: Cyber Mission Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: CMSs defend Coast Guard and maritime networks against cyber threats.

specialties.name: Damage Controlman
specialties.what_it_is: DCs perform welding, plumbing, and carpentry, and respond to shipboard fires and flooding.

specialties.name: Diver
specialties.what_it_is: DVs conduct underwater searches, recoveries, investigations, maintenance, and repairs.

specialties.name: Electrician's Mate
specialties.what_it_is: EMs install, maintain, and repair electrical equipment and machinery control systems at Coast Guard units and onboard cutters.

specialties.name: Electronics Technician
specialties.what_it_is: ETs install, repair, and maintain command, control, computer, communications, cyber, intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (C5ISR) systems.

specialties.name: Gunner's Mate
specialties.what_it_is: GMs maintain firearms, from handguns to ship-mounted guns.

specialties.name: Health Services Technician
specialties.what_it_is: HSs provide emergency healthcare to those in distress and give medical care to Coast Guard crews.

specialties.name: Information Systems Technician
specialties.what_it_is: ITs are system administrators who support the telephones, computers, and network systems used for Coast Guard missions.

specialties.name: Intelligence Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: ISs analyze information to produce intelligence products that support Coast Guard missions.

specialties.name: Machinery Technician
specialties.what_it_is: MKs maintain and repair machinery such as hydraulics and engines.

specialties.name: Marine Science Technician
specialties.what_it_is: MSTs inspect vessels and facilities and respond to pollution incidents.

specialties.name: Maritime Enforcement Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: MEs perform law enforcement duties and train others in law enforcement operations.

specialties.name: Operations Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: OSs provide continuous communication and coordination to execute Coast Guard missions.

specialties.name: Public Affairs Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: PAs communicate Coast Guard activities to the public and news media.

specialties.name: Storekeeper
specialties.what_it_is: SKs handle logistics, purchasing, and contract management.

specialties.name: Yeoman
specialties.what_it_is: YNs handle military human resources matters including pay, travel, and assignments, and some YNs provide legal-administrative support to Coast Guard Judge Advocates.

sources.title: Enlisted Careers | United States Coast Guard (main listing, self-reported "All results: 21 careers")
Maritime Enforcement Specialist (ME) — individual rating page, spot-checked for HTTP 200
Cyber Mission Specialist (CMS) — individual rating page, spot-checked for HTTP 200
Diver (DV) — individual rating page, spot-checked for HTTP 200
Information Systems Technician (IT) — individual rating page, spot-checked for HTTP 200
Avionics Electrical Technician (AET) — individual rating page, spot-checked for HTTP 200

unverified: ASVAB per-rating requirements: the Coast Guard sums raw ASVAB subtest standard scores per rating per COMDTINST M1510.10 Enclosure 3. This document was not fetched this session (not attempted — no public URL for it was located), so every entry's asvab field is null rather than a guessed line-score composite.

Clearance level per rating: not stated on any of the fetched gocoastguard.com pages. IS (Intelligence Specialist) almost certainly requires at least a Secret clearance by analogy to every other branch's intelligence-analysis rating, but this is an inference, not a sourced fact, so clearance is null rather than filled in. To verify: check the IS rating's official 'qualifications' detail (not present on the summary page fetched) or COMDTINST 1000.2 series.

16 of the 21 individual rating detail pages (https://www.gocoastguard.com/careers/enlisted/<abbr>/) were not individually fetched for an HTTP 200 spot-check — only amt, ast, aet, bm, cs, cms, dc, dv, em, et, gm, hs, it, is, mk, mst, me, os, pa, sk, yn hrefs were all directly extracted from the live, 200-status main listing page's HTML (not guessed or inferred), and 5 of the 21 (me, cms, dv, it, aet) were additionally live-fetched and confirmed 200. The remaining 16 are treated as reliable because they came from real hrefs on the official site, not from a pattern guess.

U.S. Coast Guard Band / Musician: confirmed NOT to appear among the 21 ratings on the official gocoastguard.com enlisted listing (which explicitly states 'All results: 21 careers'). This corroborates interest-taxonomy.json's existing flag that Coast Guard Band billets are audition-based and not a standalone numbered enlisted rating; no entry was fabricated for it in this catalog.
completion_accountability: 21 of 21 Coast Guard enlisted ratings catalogued: AMT, AST, AET, BM, CS, CMS, DC, DV, EM, ET, GM, HS, IT, IS, MK, MST, ME, OS, PA, SK, YN. Source is the official gocoastguard.com/careers/enlisted/ listing page, which self-reports 'All results: 21 careers' — this is a direct completeness confirmation from the source itself, not an assumption. Every entry mapped to at least one of the 14 interest-taxonomy.json cluster ids (YN mapped to two: business-admin and legal, mirroring interest-taxonomy.json's own existing dual treatment of YN). No rating abbreviation was invented; all 21 abbreviations and full names were extracted verbatim from the listing page's HTML (h3 headings pairing full name with parenthetical abbreviation). ASVAB is null for all 21 entries per the binding constraint (Coast Guard has no ASVAB line-score system; COMDTINST M1510.10 Enclosure 3 was not fetchable this session). Clearance is null for all 21 entries — no per-rating clearance data was present on any fetched page. Web calls used this session for the Coast Guard portion: 1 full-page fetch (main listing) + 5 individual-page HTTP-200 spot checks = 6.
